_readme: Published worked-example fixtures for the FS3 flash. ARMED 2026-07-19 from fetched copies of the printed sources (owner had no printed pages; sourcing rule honored by reading fetched documents, never from model memory). CASE 12 fixtures: Whitson & Brule, Phase Behavior, SPE Monograph 20 (2000), Appendix B Problem 18 - a converged PR flash with the component properties printed in Table B-28 (kij = 0 as printed), read from the full-text copy at https://pdfcoffee.com/download/whitson-pvt-spe-phase-behavior-monograph-pdf-free.html and re-verified internally (z = Fv*y + (1-Fv)*x reproduces the feed; converged fL = fV printed per component). Fixtures with `comps` are run on the PRINTED properties and `bipZero` runs kij = 0, so the gate reproduces the page, not a library-property variant. Note: the printed solution uses the PR78 cubic kappa for C10 (problem statement applies it for omega > 0.4; the engine switches at 0.491) - tolerance notes in tools/validation/fluidstudio/README.md.

comps.optional: per-key printed {mw,tcR,pcPsia,omega,shift}; omit to use the FS1 library

flashes.bipZero: optional true = all kij zero as printed

flashes.citation: Whitson & Brule, Phase Behavior, SPE Monograph 20 (2000), App. B Problem 18(a), Tables B-28/B-30: converged PR flash of C1/C4/C10 at 280F/500 psia, kij=0, Fv=0.853401
Whitson & Brule, Phase Behavior, SPE Monograph 20 (2000), App. B Problem 18(b), Tables B-28/B-32: converged PR flash of C1/C4/C10 at 280F/1500 psia after Michelsen stability test, kij=0, Fv=0.566844

coatsSmart._readme: Coats & Smart, SPE 11197 (SPE Reservoir Engineering, May 1986), Table 1 'Fluid Compositions and Properties at Reservoir Conditions'. ARMED 2026-07-19 from the scanned paper itself, publicly hosted at https://www.ipt.ntnu.no/~curtis/courses/Resevoir-Simulation/Keith-Coats-Publications/spe11197-Coats-Application-of-a-Regression-Based-EOS-PVT-Program-to-Laboratory-Data.pdf (read at 400-600 dpi; every composition column sums to exactly 1.0000; every Table 1 Ps in psig cross-attested inside the paper against Table 12 / body text psia values at exactly +14.7). Mapping: the paper's lumped C4/C5/C6 are run as nC4/nC5/nC6; the daggered plus fraction is the C7+ pseudo with the printed M+ and gamma+. Excluded: Oil 3 (C10+ plus with discrete C7-C9 not in the FS1 library) and Gas 4 (C6+ plus fraction). These are MEASURED lab saturation pressures gated against an UNTUNED PR78 with a single-pseudo C7+, so tolerances are correlation-level by design; observed errors are recorded in README.md.

fluids.citation: Coats & Smart SPE 11197, Oil/Gas N, table/page
Coats & Smart SPE 11197 Table 1, Oil 1 (180F, Ps 2520 psig = 2535 psia; heaviest fluid, C7+ SG 0.90 near-aromatic - untuned engine observed +10.3%, tolerance set just above to regression-pin)
Coats & Smart SPE 11197 Table 1, Oil 2 (176F, Ps 4460 psig = 4475 psia)
Coats & Smart SPE 11197 Table 1, Oil 4 (250F, Ps 2547 psig = 2562 psia)
Coats & Smart SPE 11197 Table 1, Oil 6 (234F, Ps 2746 psig = 2761 psia)
Coats & Smart SPE 11197 Table 1, Oil 7 (131F, Ps 1694 psig = 1709 psia)
Coats & Smart SPE 11197 Table 1, Gas 2 bubblepoint sample (190F, Ps 4415 psig = 4430 psia)
Coats & Smart SPE 11197 Table 1, Gas 2 dewpoint sample (190F, Ps 4450 psig = 4465 psia)

Coats & Smart SPE 11197 Table 1, Gas 5 dewpoint sample (267F, Ps 4842 psig = 4857 psia; lean condensate - dew point controlled by the heavy tail a single C7+ pseudo cannot resolve, untuned engine observed -15.8%, tolerance set just above to regression-pin)

separatorTests._readme: Good Oil Co. Oil Well No. 4 reservoir fluid study (Core Laboratories RFL 88001, Productive Field, Samson County, Texas - the report McCain and Ahmed reproduce). ARMED 2026-07-19 from the original report scan hosted at https://blasingame.engr.tamu.edu/z_zCourse_Archive/P324_06A/P324_06A_w_Work/Prob_02_P324_06A_Course_Work_(Ref_Good_Oil_No_4).pdf cross-verified against Whitson & Brule Phase Behavior Ch. 6 Tables 6.4/6.7/6.9 (https://www.ipt.ntnu.no/~curtis/courses/PVT-Flow/2018-TPG4145/e-notes/PVT-Papers/SPEPBM-Ch6.pdf) and wiki.whitson.com - wellstream mol%, C7+ MW/SG, all four two-stage separator tests and Pb identical across sources. Pressures in the report are PSIG (report base pressure 14.65 psia; the monograph table header's '(psia)' contradicts its own 'Gauge' footnote and both other sources) - converted here as psig + 14.65. Lab stock tank was 0 psig / 75F with volumes corrected to 60F; the engine's implicit stock-tank stage (14.696 psia / 60F) stands in for that correction. Total GOR = sum of the report's stage GORs on the 60F-STB basis (scf at 14.65/60 per STB); Bofb = bbl oil at 2620 psig / 220F per STB. Measured lab data vs UNTUNED single-pseudo PR78: correlation-level tolerances by design, observed errors recorded in README.md.

fluids.citation: Good Oil Co. Well No. 4 (Core Labs RFL 88001 p.12 / Whitson & Brule Table 6.7), separator test 50 psig & 75F to stock tank (untuned J-Y volume shift reads STO ~9 API heavy - pure C7+ pseudo std-condition SG recovers 0.9075 vs defined 0.8515; API tolerance set just above the observed bias to regression-pin, EOS tuning initiative to tighten)

Good Oil Co. Well No. 4 (Core Labs RFL 88001 p.12 / Whitson & Brule Table 6.7), separator test 100 psig & 75F to stock tank (optimum pressure row) (untuned J-Y volume shift reads STO ~9 API heavy - pure C7+ pseudo std-condition SG recovers 0.9075 vs defined 0.8515; API tolerance set just above the observed bias to regression-pin, EOS tuning initiative to tighten)

Good Oil Co. Well No. 4 (Core Labs RFL 88001 p.12 / Whitson & Brule Table 6.7), separator test 300 psig & 75F to stock tank (untuned J-Y volume shift reads STO ~9 API heavy - pure C7+ pseudo std-condition SG recovers 0.9075 vs defined 0.8515; API tolerance set just above the observed bias to regression-pin, EOS tuning initiative to tighten)
